question.text: In an ordinary annuity, payments are made:

answer.text: At the beginning of each period
At the end of each period
In the middle of each period
In a lump sum at the end of the total period

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An ordinary annuity has payments at the end of each period. This is the most common type — bond coupons, loan EMIs, and most regular payments follow this pattern. **The Trap:** Annuity due has payments at the beginning. Don't confuse the two — the timing affects valuation significantly. **Cross-Exam Context:** Annuity types are tested in X-A, XVII (Retirement Adviser), and IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Most financial products use ordinary annuity (end-of-period payments). Rent is a common example of annuity due (paid at the start of the month).
